[2018-06-04 15:24] miccull. (Miccull): is smorcus even good ? i dont like it
[2018-06-04 15:29] shadourow (Rando): I think that smorcus is really good to transition from red witout wasting red, after a classic P2 DD/DA/TTD
[2018-06-04 15:34] leary93: Smorcus is pretty good, not that inefficient as a 1attacker, but its generally misused a lot. People just too often build it as their first attacker and then go into tarsiers, while in reality its better to build the other way around, unless your opponent did not get a forge.   Thats how I feel at least, I dont often feel like I lose vs such smorcus lines.

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-06-04) ---
[2018-06-04 22:31] miuna6933 (Aka): on turn 12 if i go W pact infusion E, click phage float 2, can my opponent do anything to stop me clicking phage and buying centurion the next turn?
[2018-06-04 22:34] amalloy: i don't think they can even come close. they're having enough trouble just staying alive; they can't add a bunch of pressure
[2018-06-04 22:41] miuna6933 (Aka): does pact become better the more absorb i have?
[2018-06-04 22:43] miuna6933 (Aka): or is it more along the lines of pact is better when i have more attack relative to their defense buying ability
[2018-06-04 22:46] liadahlia (Lia): i don't think absorb changes how good soak is
[2018-06-04 22:48] Deleted User (Deleted User): pact is better the higher inflation for you is
[2018-06-04 22:48] Deleted User (Deleted User): it's also good the lower the inflation for your opponent is, but less important
[2018-06-04 22:49] miuna6933 (Aka): what exactly is inflation?
[2018-06-04 22:49] liadahlia (Lia): that's a great question
[2018-06-04 22:49] Deleted User (Deleted User): How much you want stuff/resources now vs later
[2018-06-04 22:50] amalloy: are those two things often different? it seems like if inflation is high for me, it must also mean it's high for my opponent, because it's good for him to deny me things right away
[2018-06-04 22:50] Deleted User (Deleted User): breachproof is an example of low inflation
[2018-06-04 22:50] Deleted User (Deleted User): being really breach vulnerable and/or needing defense/gold is an example of high inflation
[2018-06-04 22:51] Deleted User (Deleted User): if they just antima'd or you just arms race'd it's low inflation for them
[2018-06-04 22:51] Deleted User (Deleted User): if they pressure you it's high inflation for you
[2018-06-04 22:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): there's still the delay tho
[2018-06-04 22:51] liadahlia (Lia): so you're saying when your opponent finished antima blood pact is better?
[2018-06-04 22:52] masn6811 (Masne98888): as well as sometimes its difficult to convert resources to pressure
[2018-06-04 22:52] liadahlia (Lia): don't think that's the case
[2018-06-04 22:52] liadahlia (Lia): i think we should stop using the buzzword "inflation"
[2018-06-04 22:52] Deleted User (Deleted User): it is, if it's also high inflation for you, which it generally isn't
[2018-06-04 22:52] masn6811 (Masne98888): like inflation for both players won't really be too far apart in most cases (BP and big red maybe)
[2018-06-04 22:52] liadahlia (Lia): it means different things depending on who says it
[2018-06-04 22:52] masn6811 (Masne98888): but it can be different between the players
[2018-06-04 22:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): like BP has the lowest inflations, while big red is the highest. (early game in a high soak set is almost as low as the gauss cannon mirror, and sometimes BP inlfation can be higher if there's vulnerable units like 5 hp venges)
[2018-06-04 22:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): if I do say wincer+kit it would be really high inflation for the opp, but it would still be a relatively low inflation for me
[2018-06-04 22:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): cooledcannon has a great example there
[2018-06-04 22:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): however, if say gauss charge was in the set, the inflation for him would get higher, because he can more efficiently convert his resources to his opponent's inflation
[2018-06-04 22:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): and this is all assuming the opponent doesn't have a BP transition
[2018-06-04 22:56] miuna6933 (Aka): so in zoo expert the player has really high inflation because you want to get rid of drones to get more swords?
[2018-06-04 22:56] masn6811 (Masne98888): yeah, swords are always worth more in red mirrors, esp when you're breaching
[2018-06-04 22:57] masn6811 (Masne98888): so you want more swords now
[2018-06-04 22:57] masn6811 (Masne98888): or just swords in general, really
[2018-06-04 22:57] masn6811 (Masne98888): since gold represents buying swords through the future, it's worth less
[2018-06-04 22:57] Deleted User (Deleted User): what's zoo expert again? but yeah what MasN said sounds super right
[2018-06-04 22:57] shadourow (Rando): The lucina/fang mission
[2018-06-04 22:57] miuna6933 (Aka): it's sorta red breachproof mirror
[2018-06-04 22:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): ah yes that's pretty high inflation when attack reaches a tipping point
[2018-06-04 22:58] miuna6933 (Aka): kill their tarsiers before they kill yours
[2018-06-04 22:58] masn6811 (Masne98888): basically, Swords are worth a lot. Swords/turn filters through inflation once, but it's the best you can get. Gold/turn (which is drones) can buy (Swords/Turn)/Turn, so it get's filtered twice, and that's pretty bad
[2018-06-04 22:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): like holding drones is generally only good in high inflation(sometimes u hold 1-2 in low-mid inflation, but you'd prefer not to)

[2018-06-05 01:40] cogito3 (Cogito): @1m20 looked over the game you posted earlier. It's a complicated game so it's hard to say precisely where things went wrong, but I think part of the issue is that since you cut so many drones early, your opponent was able to get a 2nd blastforge a lot quicker (turn 5 vs turn 8). As a result they were able to get defense quicker, whereas you had to hold your splitter several times, reducing/negating your attack advantage. Then on turn 15, after your opponent bought a 2nd gauss cannon, equalizing attack, you built a 3rd blastforge (a lot worse than the 2nd conduit in this set, though you prob bought it b/c you were lacking defense) and sacrificed your granulity, which is basically where the game was sealed imo.

[2018-06-08 03:10] extramohumohulowtier (Fuzzy): What units should you aim for to punish Odin rush as P2 in Odin sets?
[2018-06-08 03:10] extramohumohulowtier (Fuzzy): I’ve read that Odin is super p1 favored unit, but
[2018-06-08 03:15] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): Idk. Maybe a preemptive wall? Or try to go for other strats instead of mirroring odin?
[2018-06-08 03:20] willflame: rush first and don't allow them to get enough gold, probably
[2018-06-08 03:22] extramohumohulowtier (Fuzzy): Oh yeah, I figured also going Odin was a losing play
[2018-06-08 03:22] extramohumohulowtier (Fuzzy): Cheap red sounds good but maybe Tarsier is too slow?
[2018-06-08 03:24] extramohumohulowtier (Fuzzy): You need like vores or something. I think if they are trying to spend 3 blue on Not-Odin it gets ugly for them and you can pressure them into a loss. Or that’s my theory anyway. I haven’t played nearly enough games :p
[2018-06-08 03:25] willflame: the longer you delay their Odin, the more time they spend trying to find use of blue tech, which is typically expensive
[2018-06-08 03:26] extramohumohulowtier (Fuzzy): Yeah exactly, it’s really hard to spend that much Blue (unless Pixie is in the set, oops)
[2018-06-08 03:35] extramohumohulowtier (Fuzzy): Yeah Drake let’s them split down the middle too
